title: rosa × dumetorum f. campicola
intro: Rosa × dumetorum f. campicola, also known as Rosa rugosa, is a hybrid species of rose native to eastern Asia, characterized by its pink to white fragrant flowers and dark green leaves. The medicinal properties of Rosa × dumetorum f. campicola lie in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Rosa × dumetorum f. campicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, colds, and fever.
